commands.review_summary:
The branch broadens cron persisted-store quarantine preservation and adds a Control UI schedule-kind guard plus regression tests for malformed cron rows.

PR surface: Source -5, Tests +119. Total +114 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main skips invalid persisted cron rows from runtime and only preserves a narrow unsupported-payload subset, while the UI list filter dereferences schedule.kind directly if a malformed job reaches the browser. I did not run a live repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility-sensitive persisted-store semantics plus normal rebase/check handling.

target.title: fix(cron): ignore malformed persisted jobs
target.body:
## Summary

- Closes #51871.
- Quarantine malformed persisted cron rows into `jobs-quarantine.json` before sanitizing active `jobs.json`, so agents that later rewrite cron config keep only canonical valid jobs without silently deleting recoverable raw rows.
- Preserve split runtime metadata (`state`, `updatedAtMs`, `scheduleIdentity`) plus original `sourceIndex`, including duplicate malformed rows and legacy `jobId` rows.
- Block later cron persists until pending quarantine succeeds; after it succeeds, force a full config/state sanitize even for state-only callers.
- Report quarantine files from `openclaw doctor`, and document the runtime quarantine/doctor review path.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/cron/service/store.test.ts src/cron/service/store.load-missing-session-target.test.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/commands/doctor-cron-store-migration.test.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/cron-filters.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo && node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/cron/store.ts src/cron/service/store.ts src/cron/service/state.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/service/store.test.ts src/cron/service/store.load-missing-session-target.test.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts src/commands/doctor-cron-store-migration.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cron/store.ts src/cron/store.test.ts src/cron/service/store.ts src/cron/service/state.ts src/cron/service/store.test.ts src/cron/service/store.load-missing-session-target.test.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.ts src/commands/doctor-cron.test.ts src/commands/doctor-cron-store-migration.test.ts docs/automation/cron-jobs.md docs/cli/cron.md docs/gateway/doctor.md`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm check:changed` reached core lint but failed because `tsgolint` segfaulted while parsing `node_modules/openai/index.d.mts`; targeted lint/type/tests above passed after that.
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed persisted cron rows are automatically removed from the active runtime store only after they are copied into the quarantine sidecar, while valid jobs continue to load and persist normally.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, with temporary cron stores in Vitest and focused cron service/store tests covering malformed rows, duplicate rows, legacy `jobId`, split state, quarantine failure recovery, and doctor reporting.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above were run after the final patch.

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the Gateway repro plus focused tests. The live `openclaw cron list --json` output showed only the valid job after the malformed row was skipped/quarantined:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/.../openclaw.json pnpm openclaw cron list --json --url ws://127.0.0.1:18890 --token <redacted>
{
  "jobs": [
    {
      "id": "job-ok",
      "name": "Daily",
      "payload": { "kind": "systemEvent", "text": "ping" },
      "state": { "nextRunAtMs": 1779699600000 }
    }
  ],
  "total": 1,
  "hasMore": false
}

cron: sanitized active jobs.json after quarantining malformed persisted jobs
```

Focused tests also assert active `jobs.json` contains only valid jobs after successful quarantine, `jobs-quarantine.json` keeps the malformed raw rows and runtime metadata, repeated failures do not rewrite or spam warnings, and doctor reports quarantined rows for manual review.

Observed result after fix: valid cron jobs remain usable; malformed persisted rows are quarantined outside active runtime config; later agent/config writes cannot delete malformed rows before quarantine succeeds.

What was not tested: a fresh browser screenshot/video of the Control UI was not captured for this rewrite; UI coverage is through the existing cron controller tests included above.
target.author: yfge
